bullets.text: Full-year CR 95.6, meeting the ≤96 target despite record catastrophe year — 28 declared catastrophes including Superstorm Sandy; post-tax underwriting profit $461M; net income $902M ($1.48/share); comprehensive ROE 17.4%.

NPW grew 8% to clear $16B — adding $1.23B in written premiums, largest absolute dollar growth that year; both written and earned premiums cleared $16B; Personal Lines PIF growth 3.7% YOY (5.2% 3-year CAGR).

Investment portfolio total return 6.8% (~$700M post-tax); preferred stock appreciation from crisis-era marks drove fixed-income returns; pretax book yield just under 3%; $1.00/share dividend paid in November plus $0.28/share variable dividend declared; $174M in share repurchases (8.6M shares).

Severity increases confirmed by mid-year after initially appearing temporary alongside elevated frequency; underwriting results slipped to 97.6 quarterly CR; rate revision response fully deployed in H2 recovering to 96.2 in Q3 and 94.6 in Q4; reaction time acknowledged as too slow.

Snapshot UBI program annual premium topped $1 billion; 57% consumer awareness and 82% Snapshot/Progressive attribution; 4th–6th Snapshot patents awarded in 2012; cumulative driving miles 'several additional round trips to Mars' beyond prior year; Big Data initiative launched with CEO oversight.

Progressive Home Advantage expanded; ASI non-controlling interest acquired in 2012 to deepen home+auto bundling integration with independent agents; multi-product customers reached low teens as percentage of book; mobile capabilities advanced to approximate desktop functionality.

Claims organization achieved lowest-ever LAE run rate while maintaining highest quality scores and strong customer satisfaction — rare combination sustained over long-term operational improvements; 94% of Sandy claims resolved before year-end.

Flo added to Ad Age 'Top Ten Female Ad Icons of All Time'; Superstore campaign continued expanding boundaries while keeping character fresh; advertising reduced in H2 during rate-revision period — media spend only slightly ahead of prior year in total.

Rate revision infrastructure in full response mode in H2 after severity confirmed; 96 CR target described as a cultural constant and business model keystone since 1971 IPO, not a variable to optimize; market competitiveness constrained by competitors' actions being closely monitored via conversion rates.

Close to 1,300 catastrophe reservists deployed in 2012; ~500,000 hours dedicated to helping Sandy and catastrophe customers; Net Promoter Scores extremely high post-catastrophe response; Progressive culture described as 'designed to consistently produce extraordinary customer experiences and economic results.'
